title: acer rubrum subsp. carolinianum
intro: Acer rubrum subsp. carolinianum, also known as the Sweet Red Maple, is a subspecies of the Red Maple tree, native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its bark, leaves, and roots. The health benefits of Acer rubrum subsp. carolinianum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat digestive issues, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to soothe sore muscles and skin irritations.
